collection: Papers from across the sciences that concluded today's AI can't do some task. We re-run each on current models, on the paper's own terms.
submit_cta: Read a paper that says today's AI can't match a human expert or an established tool? Its models may already be out of date. Nominate it and we'll re-run it on today's models.
submit_note: Thank you to the authors who open-source their benchmarks and code. That is what makes a re-run possible, and it is what science is supposed to be.

papers.claim_short: Frontier models post high scores on medical image benchmarks but stay brittle under stress, not yet ready for clinical use.
Best LLM ranked the diagnosis first in 23.6% of cases, against 35.5% for Exomiser. LLMs have not caught the traditional tool.
LLM science agents ignore evidence in 68% of traces and rarely revise beliefs. They don't reason scientifically.
